msg: warning: Variable reserved is a dead store!
reasoning: Line 30 is 'reserved = 1;' inside #ifdef CONFIG_QUOTA_DEBUG. 'reserved' is declared and initialized to 0 at line 17 (also under CONFIG_QUOTA_DEBUG) and may be set to 1 at line 30 when inode_get_rsv_space() returns > 0. However, reserved is never read anywhere in the function after either assignment — it is computed but never consumed. This is a genuine dead store. The presence condition def(CONFIG_QUOTA_DEBUG) correctly matches the #ifdef guard enclosing both the declaration (line 17) and the assignment (line 30). The vbdb entry for d7e9711760a documents the double-lock deadlock bug (lock acquired in the loop then re-acquired inside inode_get_rsv_space under CONFIG_QUOTA_DEBUG), not the dead store of reserved. New true positive.
